texto: Both of you be safe," Nessa said, looking to Turner. "Welcome to my messed up home, by the way.""Messed up?" Turner asked. "They seem like a loving bunch, all willing to kill each other off. What's family if there isn't any drama?" Turner winked at Nessa, and she nodded in reply. Drama was what the sidhe were all about.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a B. Kristin McMichael: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “Both of you be safe," Nessa said, looking to Turner. "Welcome to my messed up home, by the way.""…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, humorous, turner pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

frase_original: Both of you be safe," Nessa said, looking to Turner. "Welcome to my messed up home, by the way.""Messed up?" Turner asked. "They seem like a loving bunch, all willing to kill each other off. What's family if there isn't any drama?" Turner winked at Nessa, and she nodded in reply. Drama was what the sidhe were all about.

texto: At the end of the week, you told me that you were going on a long trip, but someday you would come back and marry me.”Arianna giggled. “Did I really say that?” she asked, mortified at her bold younger self.“Yes, but I suppose it doesn’t count if you don’t remember. Oh yeah, not to mention the fact you told two of the butlers, three maids, and your favorite cook you wanted to marry them also
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de B. Kristin McMichael que provoca temas ligados a amor. Ao questionar “At the end of the week, you told me that you were going on a long trip, but someday you would com…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, cute, marry, com foco em casamento e termino.
